name: Urban League Of Metropolitan Saint Louis, Inc.
description_short: Non-profit organization providing free financial literacy, housing counseling, employment services, and community empowerment programs to St. Louis residents since 1918.
description_long:
The Urban League of Metropolitan St. Louis, Inc. is a longstanding non-profit organization that has served the St. Louis community for 108 years, operating as the largest affiliate of the National Urban League movement. Founded in 1918, the organization is mission-driven to empower African Americans and others throughout the region in securing economic self-reliance, social equality, and civil rights under the leadership of President & CEO Michael P. McMillan.

The organization offers a comprehensive suite of free and low-cost programs spanning education, employment, housing, financial literacy, and community services. Key offerings include HUD-certified housing counseling, free employment services and job skills training reaching over 5,000 local residents annually, Head Start early childhood education programs, the Low Income Home Energy Assistance Program (LIHEAP), weatherization assistance, financial literacy programs including Family Financial Empowerment and Save Our Sisters initiatives, and specialized employment programs like Save Our Sons (targeting economically disadvantaged men) and SkillUP training.

The organization distinguishes itself through its dual focus on immediate assistance and long-term economic empowerment. Beyond direct services, ULSTL operates advocacy initiatives (Civil Rights & Advocacy division), community crisis response programs (including gun violence de-escalation and opioid triage networks), and volunteer engagement opportunities. The organization also maintains a Women's Business Center and Business Training Center, positioning itself as a comprehensive community development institution rather than a single-service provider.

As a legitimate 501(c)(3) non-profit with over a century of community presence, ULSTL is appropriate for individuals seeking genuinely free financial counseling and housing guidance without commercial motives. The primary limitation is geographic specificity—services are limited to the St. Louis metropolitan region. Additionally, while programs are free, availability and wait times are not specified on the website, and the organization's capacity relative to community demand is unclear.
pros: HUD-certified housing counseling program providing professional guidance without fees
Free employment services and job skills training reaching 5,000+ residents annually
LIHEAP (Low Income Home Energy Assistance Program) providing direct utility bill assistance
Weatherization Assistance Program enabling permanent energy bill reduction for low-income families
Comprehensive financial literacy programs including Family Financial Empowerment and Save Our Sisters
108-year organizational history with established community credibility and stability
Multi-sector approach addressing education, employment, housing, and economic opportunity simultaneously
cons: Services limited exclusively to St. Louis metropolitan region; not available nationally or for remote clients
Website does not specify application processes, eligibility requirements, or program wait times
No clear information about program capacity or whether services have waitlists
Limited detail on financial counseling scope—unclear whether debt management or credit building is covered
No pricing, cost structure, or specific eligibility income thresholds documented online
best_for: St. Louis residents seeking HUD-certified housing counseling and mortgage preparation services
Low-income families needing energy assistance (LIHEAP) or home weatherization programs
Job seekers in the St. Louis area requiring free employment training and career placement services
Women entrepreneurs interested in free business training and counseling through the Women's Business Center
diagnosis: Best for St. Louis-area residents needing free financial counseling, housing guidance, or employment services from a legitimate, century-old non-profit with no hidden commercial agenda. Primary caveat: services are geographically limited to the St. Louis metropolitan region only, and specific eligibility requirements and program availability are not detailed online.
services: Low Income Home Energy Assistance Program (LIHEAP)
Weatherization Assistance Program for energy efficiency
Free Employment Services and job skills training
Save Our Sons employment program for disadvantaged men
Financial Literacy and Family Financial Empowerment programs
Save Our Sisters women's economic empowerment initiative
Women's Business Center and Business Training
Utility Assistance and Food & Clothing Assistance
Gun Violence De-Escalation and Crisis Response services
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 1408 N Kingshighway Blvd, Saint Louis, MO 63113
